name: Sidebar footer identity ('Diego / Riley Roofing Co.') is readable on the dark teal sidebar
description: DashSidebar's footer renders the user's avatar, display name, business name, and a chevron. The inline styles previously used var(--fg) and var(--fg-muted) — light-theme dark colors — which were unreadable against the dark teal sidebar background. Names should be #fff; secondary biz line is rgba(255,255,255,0.6); chevron is rgba(255,255,255,0.55). Also the .sb__toggle button must be visibly styled across all routes (was unstyled on /assistant where dashboard.css doesn't load).

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Logged in. Profile has display name 'Diego' and businessName 'Riley Roofing Co.'.

preconditions: Profile snapshot: display='Diego', biz='Riley Roofing Co.', initials='DR'

steps.action: Visits each authed page in turn
steps.expect: Footer reads 'Diego' (white) over 'Riley Roofing Co.' (rgba 0.6) — both legible; chevron visible at rgba 0.55
The 28×28 collapse-toggle button to the right of the footer renders as a deliberate styled chevron (not as a tiny default-styled browser button)
Display-name text passes WCAG AA contrast against the dark teal background; biz line passes AA Large at 11px or hits AAA Normal

evaluationCriteria.check: Footer reads as one cohesive identity block on every page
User feels seen — the avatar + name combo is the visual anchor
Inline styles supersede the historical .sb__user-name CSS so behavior is consistent regardless of which page CSS loaded
Contrast meets WCAG AA; chevron has aria-hidden since the parent <a> already has a title
